channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Hostel owner in Sikar assists authorities in exposing NEET exam paper leak
item.description: In Sikar, Rajasthan, a hostel owner played a significant role in bringing to light the NEET exam paper leak. The incident involved unauthorised distribution of the examination paper before the schedul...

item.title: Heart-shaped Pool Spotted on Iceberg Near St. Anthony, N.L.
item.description: Photographer Steve Sheppard of St. Anthony, Newfoundland and Labrador, recently captured a striking image of an iceberg featuring a naturally formed heart-shaped pool on its surface. Initially intendi...

item.title: UN Nairobi expansion to make city a major global conference hub
item.description: Kenya is getting a major upgrade at the United Nations Office at Nairobi (UNON), which officials say will make Nairobi a bigger player in global diplomacy and conferencing. The UN has approved a nearl...

item.title: Western Cape High Court orders retrial in attorney disbarment due to procedural flaws
item.description: The Western Cape High Court has ordered that a case involving the potential disbarment of an attorney be returned to the Legal Practice Council's Disciplinary Committee for a fresh hearing. Judge Dani...

item.title: Passengers Confront Intoxicated K-Trans Driver After Road Accident in Kajiado
item.description: In Kajiado county, drama unfolded on Tuesday night, May 12, when a K-Trans Sacco bus veered off the road in the Kerarapon area, narrowly avoiding a serious accident. Passengers on board were traumatis...

item.title: IGP Orders POS Operators to Stay 200 Metres Away from Police Stations Amid Extortion Concerns
item.description: The Inspector-General of Police (IGP) for Nigeria don issue one internal directive wey tell say all Point of Sale (POS) operators must dey at least 200 metres away from any police station or formation...

item.title: Key Details and Measures in the 2026 Federal Budget
item.description: The 2026 federal budget, presented by Treasurer Jim Chalmers, comes amid economic pressures from global supply chain disruptions and inflation spikes linked to the Middle East conflict. With a strengt...

item.title: US Signals Potential End to Ceasefire with Iran Amid Rising Tensions
item.description: The United States has indicated it might end the month-long ceasefire with Iran and resume military strikes, according to statements from President Donald Trump and Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth. Tru...

item.title: Four Wild-Born Cheetah Cubs in Kuno National Park Found Dead, Leopard Attack Suspected
item.description: In a sad turn of events at Madhya Pradesh's Kuno National Park, four cheetah cubs, born in the wild to the female cheetah tagged KGP12, were discovered dead in their den on Tuesday. The cubs, born on ...

item.title: Prince Harry faces challenges as top trustee resigns from Invictus Games
item.description: Prince Harry has recently faced another setback in his charitable work, this time with the Invictus Games, an organisation he holds dear for supporting injured and traumatised veterans. Melloney Poole...

item.title: Comprehensive Guide to University of Nairobi School of Law Programs, Fees and Application Process
item.description: The University of Nairobi School of Law offers three main programs: Bachelor of Laws (LLB), Master of Laws (LLM), and PhD in Law, with additional specialized programs in Public International Law and E...

item.title: Tensions rise as US-Iran ceasefire faces uncertainty
item.description: US President Donald Trump has described the ongoing ceasefire with Iran as being on 'life support' following his rejection of Iran's latest counterproposal. Tehran had indicated that its military was ...

item.title: Kenya Ministries Invite Applications from Degree Holders for Ministerial Audit Committee Positions
item.description: The Ministry of Roads and Transport together with the Ministry of Information, Communications, and the Digital Economy have opened up job opportunities for Kenyan degree holders. The Ministry of ICT i...

item.title: Ramaphosa refuses to resign despite Constitutional Court ruling on Phala Phala affair
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa has stated he will not resign following a Constitutional Court ruling that found the National Assembly acted unconstitutionally by blocking a Section 89 inquiry into the 2020...

item.title: IHC reviews Adiala Jail report on family meetings of Bushra Bibi and political discussions outside jail
item.description: ISLAMABAD: The Islamabad High Court (IHC) on Tuesday sought detailed arguments regarding a petition filed by Mubashara Maneka, daughter of former first lady Bushra Bibi, which requests meetings with h...

item.title: Theoretical Study on Rapid Mars Missions Highlights Energy Limits
item.description: Recent media reports suggested that a Brazilian physicist, Marcelo de Oliveira Souza, discovered an accidental shortcut to Mars that could halve travel time. However, a closer look at his study, publi...

item.title: NSW Health Advises Former Patients of Strathfield Dentist to Undergo Blood-Borne Virus Testing
item.description: NSW Health has advised up to 5,000 former patients of Dr William Tam, a dentist who practised at Suite B, 2 Albert Road, Strathfield, to see their GP or healthcare provider for testing of blood-borne ...

item.title: Japan’s Calbee changes chip packaging amid supply issues from Middle East conflict
item.description: Japan’s leading snack manufacturer, Calbee, is facing supply issues linked to the ongoing Iran war, which has affected global oil and chemical supplies. Because of shortages in naphtha, an important r...

item.title: Alberta Elections Investigate Non-Cooperation Over Voter List Misuse by Separatist Group
item.description: Elections Alberta has confirmed that David Parker, a key organizer with the pro-separatist Centurion Project, is not cooperating with an investigation into the alleged misuse of Alberta's list of elec...

item.title: SHA Expands Cancer, Maternity and Sickle Cell Coverage for Kenyans
item.description: The Social Health Authority (SHA) has upgraded the annual cancer cover from KES 550,000 to KES 800,000, aiming to ease the financial burden for affected families in Kenya. The enhanced package now cov...
